content:
The correct answer is:

**(B) To measure galactic distances.**

Cepheid variable stars have a well-defined relationship between their luminosity and pulsation period, known as the Leavitt Law. This allows astronomers to determine their absolute brightness from the period of their variability. By comparing the absolute brightness to the observed brightness, they can calculate the distance to the Cepheid and thus measure distances to nearby galaxies, making Cepheids important "standard candles" in cosmic distance measurements.
